message:
fuzz(mcp): add mcp_request_params target driving __fuzz_parse_tool_call_request seam

Couples to the fuzz seam added in 0f02405b (feat(mcp):
add __fuzz_parse_tool_call_request seam behind #[cfg(feature = \"fuzz\")])
with a full libfuzzer harness, a new `mcp-fuzz-targets` feature,
and a five-file seed corpus.

Target file fuzz/fuzz_targets/mcp_request_params.rs:
- Bounds per-input cost at 256 KiB (MAX_INPUT_BYTES) so the
  harness stays inside libfuzzer's default budget even on adversarial
  deep-nesting input.
- Calls __fuzz_parse_tool_call_request twice on the same bytes and
  asserts the results match. This is a metamorphic oracle on top of
  the no-panic property: the parse seam must be deterministic —
  same bytes → same classification — so any caller that retries on
  ambiguous status also stays stable. Crashes or OOM from
  serde_json + the tool-specific params deserializers surface
  directly.

fuzz/Cargo.toml:
- New `mcp-fuzz-targets` feature that enables core-fuzz-targets
  (which already pulls frankenterm-core with `fuzz`) and additionally
  the `mcp` feature on frankenterm-core so the __fuzz_parse_tool_call_request
  symbol is available.
- `default = [\"core-fuzz-targets\", \"mcp-fuzz-targets\"]` so
  `cargo +nightly fuzz run mcp_request_params` works without an
  explicit --features flag, matching the pattern in the
  replay_recording_from_bytes harness.
- New [[bin]] stanza for mcp_request_params pinned to
  `required-features = [\"mcp-fuzz-targets\"]` so the harness
  doesn't accidentally link into a build that hasn't turned the
  feature on.

fuzz/corpus/mcp_request_params/ seeds (5 files, all small JSON):
- state_minimal.json — wa.state with a minimal arguments object;
  canonical happy-path shape.
- get_text_tail.json — wa.get_text with tail_lines in arguments;
  exercises GetTextParams deserialization.
- events_window.json — wa.events with a time-window filter;
  exercises EventsParams.
- reserve_request.json — wa.reserve with file-path list;
  exercises ReserveParams (has required fields, so distinct
  classification path from the null-args default-construct path).
- tx_plan_null.json — wa.tx_plan with \"arguments\":null to
  exercise the `or_default` branch that treats null as T::default()
  for the wa.tx_plan dispatcher.

mcp_tools.rs + mcp_missions.rs are pure rustfmt output: the import
reorder from bringing Mission into lexicographic order in the test
module triggered rustfmt to rewrap a couple of long-line `assert!`
invocations at ft-wxqbt:.tmp-must-not-linger call sites. No
behaviour change outside the fuzz feature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,8 +8,9 @@ edition = "2024"
 cargo-fuzz = true
 
 [features]
-default = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
+default = ["core-fuzz-targets", "mcp-fuzz-targets"]
 core-fuzz-targets = ["dep:frankenterm-core", "frankenterm-core/fuzz"]
+mcp-fuzz-targets = ["core-fuzz-targets", "frankenterm-core/mcp"]
 codec-fuzz-targets = ["dep:codec"]
 
 [dependencies]
@@ -180,5 +181,11 @@ name = "term_advance_bytes"
 path = "fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "mcp_request_params"
+path = "fuzz_targets/mcp_request_params.rs"
+doc = false
+required-features = ["mcp-fuzz-targets"]
+
 [profile.release]
 debug = 1
